title: The Lion and the Mouse
lines: A Mouse once fell into the paws of a Lion.
The poor creature trembled and begged, "Please let me go, and I will be your friend."
The Lion, being good-natured, let the Mouse go.
Some time after, the Lion was caught in the meshes of a hunter's net.
He struggled and roared, but could not free himself.
The Mouse, hearing his cries, ran to the spot.
"Be still, great Lion," said the Mouse.
With his tiny teeth the Mouse gnawed the ropes that bound the Lion.
Soon the great beast was free.
"You laughed at me," said the Mouse, "for being so small; but see, I have been able to help you."
moral: No act of kindness, however small, is ever wasted.
